prompt:
We finally retired the old bash box, and I ported our feed-mirror puller over to the new machines more or less line-for-line — it's pullfeeds.zsh now, running under zsh 5.9. It has not worked a single night since. The conf file hasn't changed, the curl flags haven't changed, this exact logic ran for two years. Last night's log:

    pullfeeds.zsh:25: no matches found: https://gw.example.test/ping?src=mirror
    pullfeeds: gateway check failed
    curl-stub: unknown option: -s --max-time 5
    pull failed: status
    curl-stub: unknown option: -s --max-time 5
    pull failed: builds
    ... every feed, same story ...
    done: 0 pulled, 5 failed

(That transcript is from the test rig, which fakes curl — but the real runs die identically.) Three things make no sense to me. The gateway URL is being treated like it's a filename pattern to search for — 'no matches found'?? It's a URL, it goes to curl, since when does the shell get an opinion about it. Then every feed fails with curl claiming '-s --max-time 5' is ONE unknown option — those are three tokens, they're in a variable the same as always. And in that same run you can see it attempted the archive and legacy feeds, which are on the skip list and were reliably excluded before the migration.

Fix pullfeeds.zsh so it behaves like the bash original did: gateway ping fetched, per-feed flags passed through as separate arguments, skip list honored, and don't break what already carries over fine — the URLs (query strings and all) and the output paths must keep reaching curl as single intact arguments. Keep it a zsh script; this has to run on the new boxes. zsh test_pullfeeds.zsh is the gate — it must pass unmodified, and leave the test file alone.
